    70  // staticPolicy is a CPU manager policy that does not change CPU
    71  // assignments for exclusively pinned guaranteed containers after the main
    72  // container process starts.
    73  //
    74  // This policy allocates CPUs exclusively for a container if all the following
    75  // conditions are met:
    76  //
    77  // - The pod QoS class is Guaranteed.
    78  // - The CPU request is a positive integer.
    79  //
    80  // The static policy maintains the following sets of logical CPUs:
    81  //
    82  //   - SHARED: Burstable, BestEffort, and non-integral Guaranteed containers
    83  //     run here. Initially this contains all CPU IDs on the system. As
    84  //     exclusive allocations are created and destroyed, this CPU set shrinks
    85  //     and grows, accordingly. This is stored in the state as the default
    86  //     CPU set.
    87  //
    88  //   - RESERVED: A subset of the shared pool which is not exclusively
    89  //     allocatable. The membership of this pool is static for the lifetime of
    90  //     the Kubelet. The size of the reserved pool is
    91  //     ceil(systemreserved.cpu + kubereserved.cpu).
    92  //     Reserved CPUs are taken topologically starting with lowest-indexed
    93  //     physical core, as reported by cAdvisor.
    94  //
    95  //   - ASSIGNABLE: Equal to SHARED - RESERVED. Exclusive CPUs are allocated
    96  //     from this pool.
    97  //
    98  //   - EXCLUSIVE ALLOCATIONS: CPU sets assigned exclusively to one container.
    99  //     These are stored as explicit assignments in the state.
   100  //
   101  // When an exclusive allocation is made, the static policy also updates the
   102  // default cpuset in the state abstraction. The CPU manager's periodic
   103  // reconcile loop takes care of rewriting the cpuset in cgroupfs for any
   104  // containers that may be running in the shared pool. For this reason,
   105  // applications running within exclusively-allocated containers must tolerate
   106  // potentially sharing their allocated CPUs for up to the CPU manager
   107  // reconcile period.
   108  type staticPolicy struct {
   109          // cpu socket topology
   110          topology *topology.CPUTopology
   111          // set of CPUs that is not available for exclusive assignment
   112          reservedCPUs cpuset.CPUSet
   113          // Superset of reservedCPUs. It includes not just the reservedCPUs themselves,
   114          // but also any siblings of those reservedCPUs on the same physical die.
   115          // NOTE: If the reserved set includes full physical CPUs from the beginning
   116          // (e.g. only reserved pairs of core siblings) this set is expected to be
   117          // identical to the reserved set.
   118          reservedPhysicalCPUs cpuset.CPUSet
   119          // topology manager reference to get container Topology affinity
   120          affinity topologymanager.Store
   121          // set of CPUs to reuse across allocations in a pod
   122          cpusToReuse map[string]cpuset.CPUSet
   123          // options allow to fine-tune the behaviour of the policy
   124          options StaticPolicyOptions
   125          // we compute this value multiple time, and it's not supposed to change
   126          // at runtime - the cpumanager can't deal with runtime topology changes anyway.
   127          cpuGroupSize int
   128  }
   129
   130  // Ensure staticPolicy implements Policy interface
   131  var _ Policy = &staticPolicy{}
   132
   133  // NewStaticPolicy returns a CPU manager policy that does not change CPU
   134  // assignments for exclusively pinned guaranteed containers after the main
   135  // container process starts.
   136  func NewStaticPolicy(logger logr.Logger, topology *topology.CPUTopology, numReservedCPUs int, reservedCPUs cpuset.CPUSet, affinity topologymanager.Store, cpuPolicyOptions map[string]string) (Policy, error) {
   137          opts, err := NewStaticPolicyOptions(cpuPolicyOptions)
   138          if err != nil {
   139                  return nil, err
   140          }
   141          err = ValidateStaticPolicyOptions(opts, topology, affinity)
   142          if err != nil {
   143                  return nil, err
   144          }
   145
   146          cpuGroupSize := topology.CPUsPerCore()
   147          logger.Info("created with configuration", "options", opts, "cpuGroupSize", cpuGroupSize)
   148
   149          policy := &staticPolicy{
   150                  topology:     topology,

    32  const (
    33          // defaultMaxAllowableNUMANodes specifies the maximum number of NUMA Nodes that
    34          // the TopologyManager supports on the underlying machine.
    35          //
    36          // At present, having more than this number of NUMA Nodes will result in a
    37          // state explosion when trying to enumerate possible NUMAAffinity masks and
    38          // generate hints for them. As such, if more NUMA Nodes than this are
    39          // present on a machine and the TopologyManager is enabled, an error will
    40          // be returned and the TopologyManager will not be loaded.
    41          defaultMaxAllowableNUMANodes = 8
    42          // ErrorTopologyAffinity represents the type for a TopologyAffinityError
    43          ErrorTopologyAffinity = "TopologyAffinityError"
    44  )

    54          return ErrorTopologyAffinity

    57  // Manager interface provides methods for Kubelet to manage pod topology hints
    58  type Manager interface {
    59          // PodAdmitHandler is implemented by Manager
    60          lifecycle.PodAdmitHandler
    61          // AddHintProvider adds a hint provider to manager to indicate the hint provider
    62          // wants to be consulted with when making topology hints
    63          AddHintProvider(logger klog.Logger, h HintProvider)
    64          // AddContainer adds pod to Manager for tracking
    65          AddContainer(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, containerID string)
    66          // RemoveContainer removes pod from Manager tracking
    67          RemoveContainer(containerID string) error
    68          // Store is the interface for storing pod topology hints
    69          Store
    70  }
    71
    72  type manager struct {
    73          //Topology Manager Scope
    74          scope Scope
    75  }
    76
    77  // HintProvider is an interface for components that want to collaborate to
    78  // achieve globally optimal concrete resource alignment with respect to
    79  // NUMA locality.
    80  type HintProvider interface {
    81          // GetTopologyHints returns a map of resource names to a list of possible
    82          // concrete resource allocations in terms of NUMA locality hints. Each hint
    83          // is optionally marked "preferred" and indicates the set of NUMA nodes
    84          // involved in the hypothetical allocation. The topology manager calls
    85          // this function for each hint provider, and merges the hints to produce
    86          // a consensus "best" hint. The hint providers may subsequently query the
    87          // topology manager to influence actual resource assignment.
    88          GetTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) map[string][]TopologyHint
    89          // GetPodTopologyHints returns a map of resource names to a list of possible
    90          // concrete resource allocations per Pod in terms of NUMA locality hints.
    91          GetPodTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod) map[string][]TopologyHint
    92          // Allocate triggers resource allocation to occur on the HintProvider after
    93          // all hints have been gathered and the aggregated Hint is available via a
    94          // call to Store.GetAffinity().
    95          Allocate(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) error
    96  }
    97
    98  // Store interface is to allow Hint Providers to retrieve pod affinity
    99  type Store interface {
   100          GetAffinity(podUID string, containerName string) TopologyHint
   101          GetPolicy() Policy
   102  }
   103
   104  // TopologyHint is a struct containing the NUMANodeAffinity for a Container
   105  type TopologyHint struct {
   106          NUMANodeAffinity bitmask.BitMask
   107          // Preferred is set to true when the NUMANodeAffinity encodes a preferred
   108          // allocation for the Container. It is set to false otherwise.
   109          Preferred bool
   110  }
   111
   112  // IsEqual checks if TopologyHint are equal
   113  func (th *TopologyHint) IsEqual(topologyHint TopologyHint) bool {
   114          if th.Preferred == topologyHint.Preferred {
   115                  if th.NUMANodeAffinity == nil || topologyHint.NUMANodeAffinity == nil {
   116                          return th.NUMANodeAffinity == topologyHint.NUMANodeAffinity
   117                  }
   118                  return th.NUMANodeAffinity.IsEqual(topologyHint.NUMANodeAffinity)
   119          }
   120          return false
   121  }
   122
   123  // LessThan checks if TopologyHint `a` is less than TopologyHint `b`
   124  // this means that either `a` is a preferred hint and `b` is not
   125  // or `a` NUMANodeAffinity attribute is narrower than `b` NUMANodeAffinity attribute.
   126  func (th *TopologyHint) LessThan(other TopologyHint) bool {
   127          if th.Preferred != other.Preferred {
   128                  return th.Preferred
   129          }
   130          return th.NUMANodeAffinity.IsNarrowerThan(other.NUMANodeAffinity)
   131  }

   135  // NewManager creates a new TopologyManager based on provided policy and scope
   136  func NewManager(topology []cadvisorapi.Node, topologyPolicyName string, topologyScopeName string, topologyPolicyOptions map[string]string) (Manager, error) {
   137          // Use klog.TODO() because we currently do not have a proper logger to pass in.
   138          // Replace this with an appropriate logger when refactoring this function to accept a logger parameter.
   139          logger := klog.TODO()
   140
   141          // When policy is none, the scope is not relevant, so we can short circuit here.
   142          if topologyPolicyName == PolicyNone {
   143                  logger.Info("Creating topology manager with none policy")
   144                  return &manager{scope: NewNoneScope()}, nil
   145          }
   146
   147          opts, err := NewPolicyOptions(logger, topologyPolicyOptions)
   148          if err != nil {
   149                  return nil, err
   150          }
   151
   152          logger.Info("Creating topology manager with policy per scope", "topologyPolicyName", topologyPolicyName, "topologyScopeName", topologyScopeName, "topologyPolicyOptions", opts)
   153
   154          numaInfo, err := NewNUMAInfo(topology, opts)
   155          if err != nil {
   156                  return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot discover NUMA topology: %w", err)
   157          }
   158
   159          if topologyPolicyName != PolicyNone && len(numaInfo.Nodes) > opts.MaxAllowableNUMANodes {
   160                  return nil, fmt.Errorf("unsupported on machines with more than %v NUMA Nodes", opts.MaxAllowableNUMANodes)
   161          }
   162
   163          var policy Policy
   164          switch topologyPolicyName {
   165
   166          case PolicyBestEffort:
   167                  policy = NewBestEffortPolicy(numaInfo, opts)
   168
   169          case PolicyRestricted:
   170                  policy = NewRestrictedPolicy(numaInfo, opts)
   171
   172          case PolicySingleNumaNode:
   173                  policy = NewSingleNumaNodePolicy(numaInfo, opts)
   174
   175          default:
   176                  return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown policy: \"%s\"", topologyPolicyName)
   177          }
   178
   179          var scope Scope
   180          switch topologyScopeName {
   181
   182          case containerTopologyScope:
   183                  scope = NewContainerScope(policy)
   184
   185          case podTopologyScope:
   186                  scope = NewPodScope(policy)
   187
   188          default:
   189                  return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown scope: \"%s\"", topologyScopeName)
   190          }
   191
   192          manager := &manager{
   193                  scope: scope,
   194          }
   195
   196          manager.initializeMetrics()
   197
   198          return manager, nil
   199  }

    61  // ManagerImpl is the structure in charge of managing Device Plugins.
    62  type ManagerImpl struct {
    63          checkpointdir string
    64
    65          endpoints map[string]endpointInfo // Key is ResourceName
    66          mutex     sync.Mutex
    67
    68          server plugin.Server
    69
    70          // activePods is a method for listing active pods on the node
    71          // so the amount of pluginResources requested by existing pods
    72          // could be counted when updating allocated devices
    73          activePods ActivePodsFunc
    74
    75          // sourcesReady provides the readiness of kubelet configuration sources such as apiserver update readiness.
    76          // We use it to determine when we can purge inactive pods from checkpointed state.
    77          sourcesReady config.SourcesReady
    78
    79          // allDevices holds all the devices currently registered to the device manager
    80          allDevices ResourceDeviceInstances
    81
    82          // healthyDevices contains all the registered healthy resourceNames and their exported device IDs.
    83          healthyDevices map[string]sets.Set[string]
    84
    85          // unhealthyDevices contains all the unhealthy devices and their exported device IDs.
    86          unhealthyDevices map[string]sets.Set[string]
    87
    88          // allocatedDevices contains allocated deviceIds, keyed by resourceName.
    89          allocatedDevices map[string]sets.Set[string]
    90
    91          // podDevices contains pod to allocated device mapping.
    92          podDevices        *podDevices
    93          checkpointManager checkpointmanager.CheckpointManager
    94
    95          // List of NUMA Nodes available on the underlying machine
    96          numaNodes []int
    97
    98          // Store of Topology Affinities that the Device Manager can query.
    99          topologyAffinityStore topologymanager.Store
   100
   101          // devicesToReuse contains devices that can be reused as they have been allocated to
   102          // init containers.
   103          devicesToReuse PodReusableDevices
   104
   105          // containerMap provides a mapping from (pod, container) -> containerID
   106          // for all containers in a pod. Used to detect pods running across a restart
   107          containerMap containermap.ContainerMap
   108
   109          // containerRunningSet identifies which container among those present in `containerMap`
   110          // was reported running by the container runtime when `containerMap` was computed.
   111          // Used to detect pods running across a restart
   112          containerRunningSet sets.Set[string]
   113
   114          // update channel for device health updates
   115          update chan resourceupdates.Update
   116  }

   131  // NewManagerImpl creates a new manager.
   132  func NewManagerImpl(topology []cadvisorapi.Node, topologyAffinityStore topologymanager.Store) (*ManagerImpl, error) {
   133          // Use klog.TODO() because we currently do not have a proper logger to pass in.
   134          // Replace this with an appropriate context when refactoring this function to accept a logger parameter.
   135          logger := klog.TODO()
   136          socketPath := pluginapi.KubeletSocket
   137          if runtime.GOOS == "windows" {
   138                  socketPath = os.Getenv("SYSTEMDRIVE") + pluginapi.KubeletSocketWindows
   139          }
   140          return newManagerImpl(logger, socketPath, topology, topologyAffinityStore)
   141  }
   142
   143  func newManagerImpl(logger klog.Logger, socketPath string, topology []cadvisorapi.Node, topologyAffinityStore topologymanager.Store) (*ManagerImpl, error) {
   144          logger.V(2).Info("Creating Device Plugin manager", "path", socketPath)
   145
   146          var numaNodes []int
   147          for _, node := range topology {
   148                  numaNodes = append(numaNodes, node.Id)
   149          }
   150
   151          manager := &ManagerImpl{
   152                  endpoints: make(map[string]endpointInfo),
   153
   154                  allDevices:            NewResourceDeviceInstances(),
   155                  healthyDevices:        make(map[string]sets.Set[string]),
   156                  unhealthyDevices:      make(map[string]sets.Set[string]),
   157                  allocatedDevices:      make(map[string]sets.Set[string]),
   158                  podDevices:            newPodDevices(),
   159                  numaNodes:             numaNodes,
   160                  topologyAffinityStore: topologyAffinityStore,
   161                  devicesToReuse:        make(PodReusableDevices),
   162                  update:                make(chan resourceupdates.Update, 100),
   163          }
   164
   165          server, err := plugin.NewServer(logger, socketPath, manager, manager)
   166          if err != nil {
   167                  return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create plugin server: %v", err)
   168          }
   169
   170          manager.server = server
   171          manager.checkpointdir, _ = filepath.Split(server.SocketPath())
   172
   173          // The following structures are populated with real implementations in manager.Start()
   174          // Before that, initializes them to perform no-op operations.
   175          manager.activePods = func() []*v1.Pod { return []*v1.Pod{} }
   176          manager.sourcesReady = &sourcesReadyStub{}
   177          checkpointManager, err := checkpointmanager.NewCheckpointManager(manager.checkpointdir)
   178          if err != nil {
   179                  return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to initialize checkpoint manager: %v", err)
   180          }
   181          manager.checkpointManager = checkpointManager
   182
   183          return manager, nil
   184  }
   185
   186  func (m *ManagerImpl) Updates() <-chan resourceupdates.Update {
   187          return m.update
   188  }

    56  // defaultReconcilePeriod is the default reconciliation period to keep all claim info state in sync.
    57  const defaultReconcilePeriod = 60 * time.Second
    58
    59  // The time that DRA drivers have to come back after being unregistered
    60  // before the kubelet removes their ResourceSlices.
    61  //
    62  // This must be long enough to actually allow stopping a pod and
    63  // starting the replacement (otherwise ResourceSlices get deleted
    64  // unnecessarily) and not too long (otherwise the time window were
    65  // pods might still get scheduled to the node after removal of a
    66  // driver is too long).
    67  //
    68  // 30 seconds might be long enough for a simple container restart.
    69  // If a DRA driver wants to be sure that slices don't get wiped,
    70  // it should use rolling updates.
    71  const defaultWipingDelay = 30 * time.Second

    79  // Manager is responsible for managing ResourceClaims.
    80  // It ensures that they are prepared before starting pods
    81  // and that they are unprepared before the last consuming
    82  // pod is declared as terminated.
    83  type Manager struct {
    84          // draPlugins manages the registered plugins.
    85          draPlugins *draplugin.DRAPluginManager
    86
    87          // cache contains cached claim info
    88          cache *claimInfoCache
    89
    90          // reconcilePeriod is the duration between calls to reconcileLoop.
    91          reconcilePeriod time.Duration
    92
    93          // activePods is a method for listing active pods on the node
    94          // so all claim info state can be updated in the reconciliation loop.
    95          activePods ActivePodsFunc
    96
    97          // sourcesReady provides the readiness of kubelet configuration sources such as apiserver update readiness.
    98          // We use it to determine when we can treat pods as inactive and react appropriately.
    99          sourcesReady config.SourcesReady
   100
   101          // KubeClient reference
   102          kubeClient clientset.Interface
   103
   104          // healthInfoCache contains cached health info
   105          healthInfoCache *healthInfoCache
   106
   107          // update channel for resource updates
   108          update chan resourceupdates.Update
   109  }

   111  // NewManager creates a new DRA manager.
   112  //
   113  // Most errors returned by the manager show up in the context of a pod.
   114  // They try to adhere to the following convention:
   115  // - Don't include the pod.
   116  // - Use terms that are familiar to users.
   117  // - Don't include the namespace, it can be inferred from the context.
   118  // - Avoid repeated "failed to ...: failed to ..." when wrapping errors.
   119  // - Avoid wrapping when it does not provide relevant additional information to keep the user-visible error short.
   120  func NewManager(logger klog.Logger, kubeClient clientset.Interface, stateFileDirectory string) (*Manager, error) {
   121          claimInfoCache, err := newClaimInfoCache(stateFileDirectory, draManagerStateFileName)
   122          if err != nil {
   123                  return nil, fmt.Errorf("create ResourceClaim cache: %w", err)
   124          }
   125
   126          healthInfoCache, err := newHealthInfoCache(logger, filepath.Join(stateFileDirectory, "dra_health_state"))
   127          if err != nil {
   128                  return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create healthInfo cache: %w", err)
   129          }
   130
   131          // TODO: for now the reconcile period is not configurable.
   132          // We should consider making it configurable in the future.
   133          reconcilePeriod := defaultReconcilePeriod
   134
   135          manager := &Manager{
   136                  cache:           claimInfoCache,
   137                  kubeClient:      kubeClient,
   138                  reconcilePeriod: reconcilePeriod,
   139                  activePods:      nil,
   140                  sourcesReady:    nil,
   141                  healthInfoCache: healthInfoCache,
   142                  update:          make(chan resourceupdates.Update, 100),
   143          }
   144
   145          return manager, nil
   146  }

   158  // Start starts the reconcile loop of the manager.
   159  func (m *Manager) Start(ctx context.Context, activePods ActivePodsFunc, getNode GetNodeFunc, sourcesReady config.SourcesReady) error {
   160          m.initDRAPluginManager(ctx, getNode, defaultWipingDelay)
   161          m.activePods = activePods
   162          m.sourcesReady = sourcesReady
   163          go wait.UntilWithContext(ctx, func(ctx context.Context) { m.reconcileLoop(ctx) }, m.reconcilePeriod)
   164          return nil
   165  }
   166
   167  // initPluginManager can be used instead of Start to make the manager useable
   168  // for calls to prepare/unprepare. It exists primarily for testing purposes.
   169  func (m *Manager) initDRAPluginManager(ctx context.Context, getNode GetNodeFunc, wipingDelay time.Duration) {
   170          m.draPlugins = draplugin.NewDRAPluginManager(ctx, m.kubeClient, getNode, m, wipingDelay)
   171  }

   173  // reconcileLoop ensures that any stale state in the manager's claimInfoCache gets periodically reconciled.
   174  func (m *Manager) reconcileLoop(ctx context.Context) {
   175          logger := klog.FromContext(ctx).WithName("dra-manager")
   176          // Only once all sources are ready do we attempt to reconcile.
   177          // This ensures that the call to m.activePods() below will succeed with
   178          // the actual active pods list.
   179          if m.sourcesReady == nil || !m.sourcesReady.AllReady() {
   180                  return
   181          }
   182
   183          // Get the full list of active pods.
   184          activePods := sets.New[string]()
   185          for _, p := range m.activePods() {
   186                  activePods.Insert(string(p.UID))
   187          }
   188
   189          // Get the list of inactive pods still referenced by any claimInfos.
   190          type podClaims struct {
   191                  uid        types.UID
   192                  namespace  string
   193                  claimNames []string
   194          }
   195          inactivePodClaims := make(map[string]*podClaims)
   196          m.cache.RLock()
   197          for _, claimInfo := range m.cache.claimInfo {
   198                  for podUID := range claimInfo.PodUIDs {
   199                          if activePods.Has(podUID) {
   200                                  continue

    40  // podContainerManagerImpl implements podContainerManager interface.
    41  // It is the general implementation which allows pod level container
    42  // management if qos Cgroup is enabled.
    43  type podContainerManagerImpl struct {
    44          // qosContainersInfo hold absolute paths of the top level qos containers
    45          qosContainersInfo QOSContainersInfo
    46          // Stores the mounted cgroup subsystems
    47          subsystems *CgroupSubsystems
    48          // cgroupManager is the cgroup Manager Object responsible for managing all
    49          // pod cgroups.
    50          cgroupManager CgroupManager
    51          // Maximum number of pids in a pod
    52          podPidsLimit int64
    53          // enforceCPULimits controls whether cfs quota is enforced or not
    54          enforceCPULimits bool
    55          // cpuCFSQuotaPeriod is the cfs period value, cfs_period_us, setting per
    56          // node for all containers in usec
    57          cpuCFSQuotaPeriod uint64
    58          // podContainerManager is the ContainerManager running on the machine
    59          podContainerManager ContainerManager
    60  }
    61
    62  // Make sure that podContainerManagerImpl implements the PodContainerManager interface
    63  var _ PodContainerManager = &podContainerManagerImpl{}
    64
    65  // Exists checks if the pod's cgroup already exists
    66  func (m *podContainerManagerImpl) Exists(pod *v1.Pod) bool {
    67          podContainerName, _ := m.GetPodContainerName(pod)
    68          return m.cgroupManager.Exists(podContainerName)
    69  }
    70
    71  // EnsureExists takes a pod as argument and makes sure that
    72  // pod cgroup exists if qos cgroup hierarchy flag is enabled.
    73  // If the pod level container doesn't already exist it is created.
    74  func (m *podContainerManagerImpl) EnsureExists(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod) error {
    75          // check if container already exist
    76          alreadyExists := m.Exists(pod)
    77          if !alreadyExists {
    78                  enforceCPULimits := m.enforceCPULimits
    79                  if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(kubefeatures.DisableCPUQuotaWithExclusiveCPUs) && m.podContainerManager.PodHasExclusiveCPUs(pod) {
    80                          logger.V(2).Info("Disabled CFS quota", "pod", klog.KObj(pod))
    81                          enforceCPULimits = false
    82                  }
    83                  enforceMemoryQoS := false
    84                  if utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(kubefeatures.MemoryQoS) &&
    85                          libcontainercgroups.IsCgroup2UnifiedMode() {
    86                          enforceMemoryQoS = true
    87                  }
    88                  // Create the pod container
    89                  podContainerName, _ := m.GetPodContainerName(pod)
    90                  containerConfig := &CgroupConfig{
    91                          Name:               podContainerName,
    92                          ResourceParameters: ResourceConfigForPod(pod, enforceCPULimits, m.cpuCFSQuotaPeriod, enforceMemoryQoS),
    93                  }
    94                  if m.podPidsLimit > 0 {
    95                          containerConfig.ResourceParameters.PidsLimit = &m.podPidsLimit
    96                  }
    97                  if enforceMemoryQoS {
    98                          logger.V(4).Info("MemoryQoS config for pod", "pod", klog.KObj(pod), "unified", containerConfig.ResourceParameters.Unified)
    99                  }
   100                  if err := m.cgroupManager.Create(logger, containerConfig); err != nil {
   101                          return fmt.Errorf("failed to create container for %v : %v", podContainerName, err)
   102                  }
   103
   104          }
   105          return nil
   106  }
   107
   108  // GetPodContainerName returns the CgroupName identifier, and its literal cgroupfs form on the host.
   109  func (m *podContainerManagerImpl) GetPodContainerName(pod *v1.Pod) (CgroupName, string) {
   110          podQOS := v1qos.GetPodQOS(pod)
   111          // Get the parent QOS container name
   112          var parentContainer CgroupName
   113          switch podQOS {
   114          case v1.PodQOSGuaranteed:
   115                  parentContainer = m.qosContainersInfo.Guaranteed
   116          case v1.PodQOSBurstable:
   117                  parentContainer = m.qosContainersInfo.Burstable
   118          case v1.PodQOSBestEffort:
   119                  parentContainer = m.qosContainersInfo.BestEffort
   120          }
   121          podContainer := GetPodCgroupNameSuffix(pod.UID)
   122
   123          // Get the absolute path of the cgroup
   124          cgroupName := NewCgroupName(parentContainer, podContainer)
   125          // Get the literal cgroupfs name
   126          cgroupfsName := m.cgroupManager.Name(cgroupName)
   127
   128          return cgroupName, cgroupfsName
   129  }

   131  func (m *podContainerManagerImpl) GetPodCgroupMemoryUsage(pod *v1.Pod) (uint64, error) {
   132          podCgroupName, _ := m.GetPodContainerName(pod)
   133          memUsage, err := m.cgroupManager.MemoryUsage(podCgroupName)
   134          if err != nil {
   135                  return 0, err
   136          }
   137          return uint64(memUsage), nil
   138  }

   150  // Kill one process ID
   151  func (m *podContainerManagerImpl) killOnePid(logger klog.Logger, pid int) error {
   152          // os.FindProcess never returns an error on POSIX
   153          // https://go-review.googlesource.com/c/go/+/19093
   154          p, _ := os.FindProcess(pid)
   155          if err := p.Kill(); err != nil {
   156                  // If the process already exited, that's fine.
   157                  if errors.Is(err, os.ErrProcessDone) {
   158                          logger.V(3).Info("Process no longer exists", "pid", pid)
   159                          return nil
   160                  }
   161                  return err
   162          }
   163          return nil
   164  }
   165
   166  // Scan through the whole cgroup directory and kill all processes either
   167  // attached to the pod cgroup or to a container cgroup under the pod cgroup
   168  func (m *podContainerManagerImpl) tryKillingCgroupProcesses(logger klog.Logger, podCgroup CgroupName) error {
   169          pidsToKill := m.cgroupManager.Pids(logger, podCgroup)
   170          // No pids charged to the terminated pod cgroup return
   171          if len(pidsToKill) == 0 {
   172                  return nil
   173          }
   174
   175          var errlist []error
   176          // os.Kill often errors out,
   177          // We try killing all the pids multiple times
   178          removed := map[int]bool{}
   179          for i := 0; i < 5; i++ {
   180                  if i != 0 {
   181                          logger.V(3).Info("Attempt failed to kill all unwanted process from cgroup, retrying", "attempt", i, "cgroupName", podCgroup)
   182                  }
   183                  errlist = []error{}
   184                  for _, pid := range pidsToKill {
   185                          if _, ok := removed[pid]; ok {
   186                                  continue
   187                          }
   188                          logger.V(3).Info("Attempting to kill process from cgroup", "pid", pid, "cgroupName", podCgroup)
   189                          if err := m.killOnePid(logger, pid); err != nil {
   190                                  logger.V(3).Info("Failed to kill process from cgroup", "pid", pid, "cgroupName", podCgroup, "err", err)
   191                                  errlist = append(errlist, err)
   192                          } else {
   193                                  removed[pid] = true
   194                          }
   195                  }
   196                  if len(errlist) == 0 {
   197                          logger.V(3).Info("Successfully killed all unwanted processes from cgroup", "cgroupName", podCgroup)
   198                          return nil
   199                  }
   200          }

    40  const (
    41          // These limits are defined in the kernel:
    42          // https://github.com/torvalds/linux/blob/0bddd227f3dc55975e2b8dfa7fc6f959b062a2c7/kernel/sched/sched.h#L427-L428
    43          MinShares = 2
    44          MaxShares = 262144
    45
    46          SharesPerCPU  = 1024
    47          MilliCPUToCPU = 1000
    48
    49          // 100000 microseconds is equivalent to 100ms
    50          QuotaPeriod = 100000
    51          // 1000 microseconds is equivalent to 1ms
    52          // defined here:
    53          // https://github.com/torvalds/linux/blob/cac03ac368fabff0122853de2422d4e17a32de08/kernel/sched/core.c#L10546
    54          MinQuotaPeriod = 1000
    55
    56          // From the inverse of the conversion in MilliCPUToQuota:
    57          // MinQuotaPeriod * MilliCPUToCPU / QuotaPeriod
    58          MinMilliCPULimit = 10
    59  )
    60
    61  // MilliCPUToQuota converts milliCPU to CFS quota and period values.
    62  // Input parameters and resulting value is number of microseconds.
    63  func MilliCPUToQuota(milliCPU int64, period int64) (quota int64) {
    64          // CFS quota is measured in two values:
    65          //  - cfs_period_us=100ms (the amount of time to measure usage across given by period)
    66          //  - cfs_quota=20ms (the amount of cpu time allowed to be used across a period)
    67          // so in the above example, you are limited to 20% of a single CPU
    68          // for multi-cpu environments, you just scale equivalent amounts
    69          // see https://www.kernel.org/doc/Documentation/scheduler/sched-bwc.txt for details
    70
    71          if milliCPU == 0 {
    72                  return
    73          }
    74
    75          if !utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(kubefeatures.CPUCFSQuotaPeriod) {
    76                  period = QuotaPeriod
    77          }
    78
    79          // we then convert your milliCPU to a value normalized over a period
    80          quota = (milliCPU * period) / MilliCPUToCPU
    81
    82          // quota needs to be a minimum of 1ms.
    83          if quota < MinQuotaPeriod {
    84                  quota = MinQuotaPeriod
    85          }
    86          return
    87  }
    88
    89  // MilliCPUToShares converts the milliCPU to CFS shares.
    90  func MilliCPUToShares(milliCPU int64) uint64 {
    91          if milliCPU == 0 {
    92                  // Docker converts zero milliCPU to unset, which maps to kernel default
    93                  // for unset: 1024. Return 2 here to really match kernel default for
    94                  // zero milliCPU.
    95                  return MinShares
    96          }
    97          // Conceptually (milliCPU / milliCPUToCPU) * sharesPerCPU, but factored to improve rounding.
    98          shares := (milliCPU * SharesPerCPU) / MilliCPUToCPU
    99          if shares < MinShares {
   100                  return MinShares
   101          }
   102          if shares > MaxShares {
   103                  return MaxShares
   104          }
   105          return uint64(shares)
   106  }
   107
   108  // HugePageLimits converts the API representation to a map
   109  // from huge page size (in bytes) to huge page limit (in bytes).
   110  func HugePageLimits(resourceList v1.ResourceList) map[int64]int64 {
   111          hugePageLimits := map[int64]int64{}
   112          for k, v := range resourceList {
   113                  if v1helper.IsHugePageResourceName(k) {
   114                          pageSize, _ := v1helper.HugePageSizeFromResourceName(k)
   115                          if value, exists := hugePageLimits[pageSize.Value()]; exists {
   116                                  hugePageLimits[pageSize.Value()] = value + v.Value()
   117                          } else {
   118                                  hugePageLimits[pageSize.Value()] = v.Value()
   119                          }
   120                  }
   121          }
   122          return hugePageLimits
   123  }
   124
   125  // ResourceConfigForPod takes the input pod and outputs the cgroup resource config.
   126  func ResourceConfigForPod(allocatedPod *v1.Pod, enforceCPULimits bool, cpuPeriod uint64, enforceMemoryQoS bool) *ResourceConfig {
   127          podLevelResourcesEnabled := utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(kubefeatures.PodLevelResources)
   128          // sum requests and limits.
   129          reqs := resourcehelper.PodRequests(allocatedPod, resourcehelper.PodResourcesOptions{
   130                  // SkipPodLevelResources is set to false when PodLevelResources feature is enabled.
   131                  SkipPodLevelResources: !podLevelResourcesEnabled,
   132                  UseStatusResources:    false,
   133          })
   134          // track if limits were applied for each resource.
   135          memoryLimitsDeclared := true
   136          cpuLimitsDeclared := true
   137
   138          limits := resourcehelper.PodLimits(allocatedPod, resourcehelper.PodResourcesOptions{
   139                  // SkipPodLevelResources is set to false when PodLevelResources feature is enabled.
   140                  SkipPodLevelResources: !podLevelResourcesEnabled,
   141                  ContainerFn: func(res v1.ResourceList, containerType resourcehelper.ContainerType) {
   142                          if res.Cpu().IsZero() {
   143                                  cpuLimitsDeclared = false
   144                          }
   145                          if res.Memory().IsZero() {
   146                                  memoryLimitsDeclared = false
   147                          }
   148                  },
   149          })

    32  // for typecheck across platforms
    33  var _ func(int64, int64) int64 = MilliCPUToQuota
    34  var _ func(int64) uint64 = MilliCPUToShares
    35  var _ func(*v1.Pod, bool, uint64, bool) *ResourceConfig = ResourceConfigForPod
    36  var _ func() (*CgroupSubsystems, error) = GetCgroupSubsystems

    41
    42  // hardEvictionReservation returns a resourcelist that includes reservation of resources based on hard eviction thresholds.
    43  func hardEvictionReservation(thresholds []evictionapi.Threshold, capacity v1.ResourceList) v1.ResourceList {
    44          if len(thresholds) == 0 {
    45                  return nil
    46          }
    47          ret := v1.ResourceList{}
    48          for _, threshold := range thresholds {
    49                  if threshold.Operator != evictionapi.OpLessThan {
    50                          continue
    51                  }
    52                  switch threshold.Signal {
    53                  case evictionapi.SignalMemoryAvailable:
    54                          memoryCapacity := capacity[v1.ResourceMemory]
    55                          value := evictionapi.GetThresholdQuantity(threshold.Value, &memoryCapacity)
    56                          ret[v1.ResourceMemory] = *value
    57                  case evictionapi.SignalNodeFsAvailable:
    58                          storageCapacity := capacity[v1.ResourceEphemeralStorage]
    59                          value := evictionapi.GetThresholdQuantity(threshold.Value, &storageCapacity)
    60                          ret[v1.ResourceEphemeralStorage] = *value
    61                  }
    62          }
    63          return ret
    64  }
    65
    66  func buildContainerMapAndRunningSetFromRuntime(ctx context.Context, runtimeService internalapi.RuntimeService) (containermap.ContainerMap, sets.Set[string]) {
    67          logger := klog.FromContext(ctx)
    68          podSandboxMap := make(map[string]string)
    69          podSandboxList, _ := runtimeService.ListPodSandbox(ctx, nil)
    70          for _, p := range podSandboxList {
    71                  podSandboxMap[p.Id] = p.Metadata.Uid
    72          }
    73
    74          runningSet := sets.New[string]()
    75          containerMap := containermap.NewContainerMap()
    76          containerList, _ := runtimeService.ListContainers(ctx, nil)
    77          for _, c := range containerList {
    78                  if _, exists := podSandboxMap[c.PodSandboxId]; !exists {
    79                          logger.Info("No PodSandBox found for the container", "podSandboxId", c.PodSandboxId, "containerName", c.Metadata.Name, "containerId", c.Id)
    80                          continue
    81                  }
    82                  podUID := podSandboxMap[c.PodSandboxId]
    83                  containerMap.Add(podUID, c.Metadata.Name, c.Id)
    84                  if c.State == runtimeapi.ContainerState_CONTAINER_RUNNING {
    85                          logger.V(4).Info("Container reported running", "podSandboxId", c.PodSandboxId, "podUID", podUID, "containerName", c.Metadata.Name, "containerId", c.Id)
    86                          runningSet.Insert(c.Id)
    87                  }
    88          }
    89          return containerMap, runningSet
    90  }

    39  type podTopologyHints map[string]map[string]TopologyHint
    40
    41  // Scope interface for Topology Manager
    42  type Scope interface {
    43          Name() string
    44          GetPolicy() Policy
    45          Admit(ctx context.Context, pod *v1.Pod) lifecycle.PodAdmitResult
    46          // AddHintProvider adds a hint provider to manager to indicate the hint provider
    47          // wants to be consoluted with when making topology hints
    48          AddHintProvider(h HintProvider)
    49          // AddContainer adds pod to Manager for tracking
    50          AddContainer(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, containerID string)

    57  type scope struct {
    58          mutex sync.Mutex

    17  package admission

    19  import (
    20          "errors"
    21          "fmt"
    22
    23          "k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/lifecycle"
    24  )
    25
    26  const (
    27          ErrorReasonUnexpected = "UnexpectedAdmissionError"
    28  )
    29
    30  type Error interface {
    31          Error() string
    32          Type() string
    33  }
    34
    35  type unexpectedAdmissionError struct{ Err error }
    36
    37  var _ Error = (*unexpectedAdmissionError)(nil)
    38
    39  func (e *unexpectedAdmissionError) Error() string {
    40          return fmt.Sprintf("Allocate failed due to %v, which is unexpected", e.Err)
    41  }
    42
    43  func (e *unexpectedAdmissionError) Type() string {
    44          return ErrorReasonUnexpected
    45  }
    46
    47  func GetPodAdmitResult(err error) lifecycle.PodAdmitResult {
    48          if err == nil {
    49                  return lifecycle.PodAdmitResult{Admit: true}
    50          }
    51
    52          var admissionErr Error
    53          if !errors.As(err, &admissionErr) {
    54                  admissionErr = &unexpectedAdmissionError{err}
    55          }
    56
    57          return lifecycle.PodAdmitResult{
    58                  Message: admissionErr.Error(),
    59                  Reason:  admissionErr.Type(),
    60                  Admit:   false,
    61          }
    62  }

    59          // the presence of alternative control groups not known to runc confuses

    62          // scoped to the set control groups it understands.  this is being discussed

    67          if _, ok := c.subsystems.MountPoints["hugetlb"]; ok {
    68                  allowlistControllers.Insert("hugetlb")
    69          }
    70          var missingPaths []string
    71          // If even one cgroup path doesn't exist, then the cgroup doesn't exist.
    72          for controller, path := range cgroupPaths {
    73                  // ignore mounts we don't care about
    74                  if !allowlistControllers.Has(controller) {
    75                          continue
    76                  }
    77                  if !libcontainercgroups.PathExists(path) {
    78                          missingPaths = append(missingPaths, path)
    79                  }
    80          }

    85
    86          return nil
    87  }

    17  package topologymanager
    18
    19  import (
    20          "context"
    21
    22          "k8s.io/api/core/v1"
    23          "k8s.io/klog/v2"
    24          "k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/admission"
    25          "k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/containermap"
    26          "k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/lifecycle"
    27          "k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/metrics"
    28  )
    29
    30  type containerScope struct {
    31          scope
    32  }
    33
    34  // Ensure containerScope implements Scope interface
    35  var _ Scope = &containerScope{}
    36
    37  // NewContainerScope returns a container scope.
    38  func NewContainerScope(policy Policy) Scope {
    39          return &containerScope{
    40                  scope{
    41                          name:             containerTopologyScope,
    42                          podTopologyHints: podTopologyHints{},
    43                          policy:           policy,
    44                          podMap:           containermap.NewContainerMap(),
    45                  },
    46          }
    47  }
    48
    49  func (s *containerScope) Admit(ctx context.Context, pod *v1.Pod) lifecycle.PodAdmitResult {
    50          logger := klog.FromContext(ctx)
    51
    52          for _, container := range append(pod.Spec.InitContainers, pod.Spec.Containers...) {
    53                  bestHint, admit := s.calculateAffinity(logger, pod, &container)
    54                  logger.Info("Best TopologyHint", "bestHint", bestHint, "pod", klog.KObj(pod), "containerName", container.Name)
    55
    56                  if !admit {
    57                          if IsAlignmentGuaranteed(s.policy) {
    58                                  metrics.ContainerAlignedComputeResourcesFailure.WithLabelValues(metrics.AlignScopeContainer, metrics.AlignedNUMANode).Inc()
    59                          }
    60                          metrics.TopologyManagerAdmissionErrorsTotal.Inc()
    61                          return admission.GetPodAdmitResult(&TopologyAffinityError{})
    62                  }
    63                  logger.Info("Topology Affinity", "bestHint", bestHint, "pod", klog.KObj(pod), "containerName", container.Name)
    64                  s.setTopologyHints(string(pod.UID), container.Name, bestHint)
    65
    66                  err := s.allocateAlignedResources(pod, &container)
    67                  if err != nil {
    68                          metrics.TopologyManagerAdmissionErrorsTotal.Inc()
    69                          return admission.GetPodAdmitResult(err)
    70                  }
    71
    72                  if IsAlignmentGuaranteed(s.policy) {
    73                          logger.V(4).Info("Resource alignment at container scope guaranteed", "pod", klog.KObj(pod))
    74                          metrics.ContainerAlignedComputeResources.WithLabelValues(metrics.AlignScopeContainer, metrics.AlignedNUMANode).Inc()
    75                  }
    76          }
    77          return admission.GetPodAdmitResult(nil)
    78  }
    79
    80  func (s *containerScope) accumulateProvidersHints(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) []map[string][]TopologyHint {
    81          var providersHints []map[string][]TopologyHint
    82
    83          for _, provider := range s.hintProviders {
    84                  // Get the TopologyHints for a Container from a provider.
    85                  hints := provider.GetTopologyHints(pod, container)
    86                  providersHints = append(providersHints, hints)
    87                  logger.Info("TopologyHints", "hints", hints, "pod", klog.KObj(pod), "containerName", container.Name)
    88          }
    89          return providersHints
    90  }
    91
    92  func (s *containerScope) calculateAffinity(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) (TopologyHint, bool) {
    93          providersHints := s.accumulateProvidersHints(logger, pod, container)
    94          bestHint, admit := s.policy.Merge(logger, providersHints)
    95          logger.Info("ContainerTopologyHint", "bestHint", bestHint, "pod", klog.KObj(pod), "containerName", container.Name)
    96          return bestHint, admit
    97  }

    41
    42  // createNodeAllocatableCgroups creates Node Allocatable Cgroup when CgroupsPerQOS flag is specified as true
    43  func (cm *containerManagerImpl) createNodeAllocatableCgroups(logger klog.Logger) error {
    44          nodeAllocatable := cm.internalCapacity
    45          // Use Node Allocatable limits instead of capacity if the user requested enforcing node allocatable.
    46          nc := cm.NodeConfig.NodeAllocatableConfig
    47          if cm.CgroupsPerQOS && nc.EnforceNodeAllocatable.Has(kubetypes.NodeAllocatableEnforcementKey) {
    48                  nodeAllocatable = cm.getNodeAllocatableInternalAbsolute()
    49          }
    50
    51          cgroupConfig := &CgroupConfig{
    52                  Name: cm.cgroupRoot,
    53                  // The default limits for cpu shares can be very low which can lead to CPU starvation for pods.
    54                  ResourceParameters: cm.getCgroupConfig(nodeAllocatable, false),
    55          }
    56          if cm.cgroupManager.Exists(cgroupConfig.Name) {
    57                  return nil
    58          }
    59          if err := cm.cgroupManager.Create(logger, cgroupConfig); err != nil {
    60                  logger.Error(err, "Failed to create cgroup", "cgroupName", cm.cgroupRoot)
    61                  return err
    62          }
    63          return nil
    64  }
    65
    66  // enforceNodeAllocatableCgroups enforce Node Allocatable Cgroup settings.
    67  func (cm *containerManagerImpl) enforceNodeAllocatableCgroups(logger klog.Logger) error {
    68          nc := cm.NodeConfig.NodeAllocatableConfig
    69
    70          // We need to update limits on node allocatable cgroup no matter what because
    71          // default cpu shares on cgroups are low and can cause cpu starvation.
    72          nodeAllocatable := cm.internalCapacity
    73          // Use Node Allocatable limits instead of capacity if the user requested enforcing node allocatable.
    74          if cm.CgroupsPerQOS && nc.EnforceNodeAllocatable.Has(kubetypes.NodeAllocatableEnforcementKey) {
    75                  nodeAllocatable = cm.getNodeAllocatableInternalAbsolute()
    76          }
    77
    78          logger.V(4).Info("Attempting to enforce Node Allocatable", "config", nc)
    79
    80          cgroupConfig := &CgroupConfig{
    81                  Name:               cm.cgroupRoot,
    82                  ResourceParameters: cm.getCgroupConfig(nodeAllocatable, false),
    83          }
    84
    85          // Using ObjectReference for events as the node maybe not cached; refer to #42701 for detail.
    86          nodeRef := nodeRefFromNode(cm.nodeInfo.Name)
    87
    88          // If Node Allocatable is enforced on a node that has not been drained or is updated on an existing node to a lower value,
    89          // existing memory usage across pods might be higher than current Node Allocatable Memory Limits.
    90          // Pod Evictions are expected to bring down memory usage to below Node Allocatable limits.
    91          // Until evictions happen retry cgroup updates.
    92          // Update limits on non root cgroup-root to be safe since the default limits for CPU can be too low.
    93          // Check if cgroupRoot is set to a non-empty value (empty would be the root container)
    94          if len(cm.cgroupRoot) > 0 {
    95                  go func() {
    96                          for {
    97                                  err := cm.cgroupManager.Update(logger, cgroupConfig)
    98                                  if err == nil {
    99                                          cm.recorder.Event(nodeRef, v1.EventTypeNormal, events.SuccessfulNodeAllocatableEnforcement, "Updated Node Allocatable limit across pods")
   100                                          return
   101                                  }
   102                                  message := fmt.Sprintf("Failed to update Node Allocatable Limits %q: %v", cm.cgroupRoot, err)
   103                                  cm.recorder.Event(nodeRef, v1.EventTypeWarning, events.FailedNodeAllocatableEnforcement, message)
   104                                  time.Sleep(time.Minute)
   105                          }
   106                  }()
   107          }
   108          // Now apply kube reserved and system reserved limits if required.
   109          if nc.EnforceNodeAllocatable.Has(kubetypes.SystemReservedEnforcementKey) {
   110                  logger.V(2).Info("Enforcing system reserved on cgroup", "cgroupName", nc.SystemReservedCgroupName, "limits", nc.SystemReserved)
   111                  if err := cm.enforceExistingCgroup(logger, nc.SystemReservedCgroupName, nc.SystemReserved, false); err != nil {
   112                          message := fmt.Sprintf("Failed to enforce System Reserved Cgroup Limits on %q: %v", nc.SystemReservedCgroupName, err)
   113                          cm.recorder.Event(nodeRef, v1.EventTypeWarning, events.FailedNodeAllocatableEnforcement, message)
   114                          return errors.New(message)
   115                  }
   116                  cm.recorder.Eventf(nodeRef, v1.EventTypeNormal, events.SuccessfulNodeAllocatableEnforcement, "Updated limits on system reserved cgroup %v", nc.SystemReservedCgroupName)
   117          }
   118          if nc.EnforceNodeAllocatable.Has(kubetypes.KubeReservedEnforcementKey) {
   119                  logger.V(2).Info("Enforcing kube reserved on cgroup", "cgroupName", nc.KubeReservedCgroupName, "limits", nc.KubeReserved)
   120                  if err := cm.enforceExistingCgroup(logger, nc.KubeReservedCgroupName, nc.KubeReserved, false); err != nil {
   121                          message := fmt.Sprintf("Failed to enforce Kube Reserved Cgroup Limits on %q: %v", nc.KubeReservedCgroupName, err)
   122                          cm.recorder.Event(nodeRef, v1.EventTypeWarning, events.FailedNodeAllocatableEnforcement, message)
   123                          return errors.New(message)
   124                  }
   125                  cm.recorder.Eventf(nodeRef, v1.EventTypeNormal, events.SuccessfulNodeAllocatableEnforcement, "Updated limits on kube reserved cgroup %v", nc.KubeReservedCgroupName)
   126          }
   127
   128          if nc.EnforceNodeAllocatable.Has(kubetypes.SystemReservedCompressibleEnforcementKey) {
   129                  logger.V(2).Info("Enforcing system reserved compressible on cgroup", "cgroupName", nc.SystemReservedCgroupName, "limits", nc.SystemReserved)
   130                  if err := cm.enforceExistingCgroup(logger, nc.SystemReservedCgroupName, nc.SystemReserved, true); err != nil {
   131                          message := fmt.Sprintf("Failed to enforce System Reserved Compressible Cgroup Limits on %q: %v", nc.SystemReservedCgroupName, err)
   132                          cm.recorder.Event(nodeRef, v1.EventTypeWarning, events.FailedNodeAllocatableEnforcement, message)
   133                          return errors.New(message)
   134                  }
   135                  cm.recorder.Eventf(nodeRef, v1.EventTypeNormal, events.SuccessfulNodeAllocatableEnforcement, "Updated limits on system reserved cgroup %v", nc.SystemReservedCgroupName)
   136          }
   137
   138          if nc.EnforceNodeAllocatable.Has(kubetypes.KubeReservedCompressibleEnforcementKey) {
   139                  logger.V(2).Info("Enforcing kube reserved compressible on cgroup", "cgroupName", nc.KubeReservedCgroupName, "limits", nc.KubeReserved)
   140                  if err := cm.enforceExistingCgroup(logger, nc.KubeReservedCgroupName, nc.KubeReserved, true); err != nil {
   141                          message := fmt.Sprintf("Failed to enforce Kube Reserved Compressible Cgroup Limits on %q: %v", nc.KubeReservedCgroupName, err)
   142                          cm.recorder.Event(nodeRef, v1.EventTypeWarning, events.FailedNodeAllocatableEnforcement, message)
   143                          return errors.New(message)
   144                  }
   145                  cm.recorder.Eventf(nodeRef, v1.EventTypeNormal, events.SuccessfulNodeAllocatableEnforcement, "Updated limits on kube reserved cgroup %v", nc.KubeReservedCgroupName)
   146          }
   147          return nil
   148  }

    42  const (
    43          // how often the qos cgroup manager will perform periodic update
    44          // of the qos level cgroup resource constraints
    45          periodicQOSCgroupUpdateInterval = 1 * time.Minute
    46  )
    47
    48  type QOSContainerManager interface {
    49          Start(context.Context, func() v1.ResourceList, ActivePodsFunc) error
    50          GetQOSContainersInfo() QOSContainersInfo
    51          UpdateCgroups(logger klog.Logger) error
    52  }
    53
    54  type qosContainerManagerImpl struct {
    55          sync.Mutex
    56          qosContainersInfo  QOSContainersInfo
    57          subsystems         *CgroupSubsystems
    58          cgroupManager      CgroupManager
    59          activePods         ActivePodsFunc
    60          getNodeAllocatable func() v1.ResourceList
    61          cgroupRoot         CgroupName
    62          qosReserved        map[v1.ResourceName]int64
    63  }
    64
    65  func NewQOSContainerManager(subsystems *CgroupSubsystems, cgroupRoot CgroupName, nodeConfig NodeConfig, cgroupManager CgroupManager) (QOSContainerManager, error) {
    66          if !nodeConfig.CgroupsPerQOS {
    67                  return &qosContainerManagerNoop{
    68                          cgroupRoot: cgroupRoot,
    69                  }, nil
    70          }
    71
    72          return &qosContainerManagerImpl{
    73                  subsystems:    subsystems,
    74                  cgroupManager: cgroupManager,
    75                  cgroupRoot:    cgroupRoot,
    76                  qosReserved:   nodeConfig.QOSReserved,
    77          }, nil
    78  }
    79
    80  func (m *qosContainerManagerImpl) GetQOSContainersInfo() QOSContainersInfo {
    81          return m.qosContainersInfo
    82  }
    83
    84  func (m *qosContainerManagerImpl) Start(ctx context.Context, getNodeAllocatable func() v1.ResourceList, activePods ActivePodsFunc) error {
    85          logger := klog.FromContext(ctx)
    86          cm := m.cgroupManager
    87          rootContainer := m.cgroupRoot
    88
    89          if err := cm.Validate(rootContainer); err != nil {
    90                  return fmt.Errorf("error validating root container %v : %w", rootContainer, err)
    91          }
    92
    93          // Top level for Qos containers are created only for Burstable
    94          // and Best Effort classes
    95          qosClasses := map[v1.PodQOSClass]CgroupName{
    96                  v1.PodQOSBurstable:  NewCgroupName(rootContainer, strings.ToLower(string(v1.PodQOSBurstable))),
    97                  v1.PodQOSBestEffort: NewCgroupName(rootContainer, strings.ToLower(string(v1.PodQOSBestEffort))),
    98          }
    99
   100          // Create containers for both qos classes
   101          for qosClass, containerName := range qosClasses {
   102                  resourceParameters := &ResourceConfig{}
   103                  // the BestEffort QoS class has a statically configured minShares value
   104                  if qosClass == v1.PodQOSBestEffort {
   105                          minShares := uint64(MinShares)
   106                          resourceParameters.CPUShares = &minShares
   107                  }
   108
   109                  // containerConfig object stores the cgroup specifications
   110                  containerConfig := &CgroupConfig{
   111                          Name:               containerName,
   112                          ResourceParameters: resourceParameters,
   113                  }
   114
   115                  // for each enumerated huge page size, the qos tiers are unbounded
   116                  m.setHugePagesUnbounded(containerConfig)
   117
   118                  // check if it exists
   119                  if !cm.Exists(containerName) {
   120                          if err := cm.Create(logger, containerConfig); err != nil {
   121                                  return fmt.Errorf("failed to create top level %v QOS cgroup : %v", qosClass, err)
   122                          }
   123                  } else {
   124                          // to ensure we actually have the right state, we update the config on startup
   125                          if err := cm.Update(logger, containerConfig); err != nil {
   126                                  return fmt.Errorf("failed to update top level %v QOS cgroup : %v", qosClass, err)
   127                          }
   128                  }
   129          }
   130          // Store the top level qos container names
   131          m.qosContainersInfo = QOSContainersInfo{
   132                  Guaranteed: rootContainer,
   133                  Burstable:  qosClasses[v1.PodQOSBurstable],
   134                  BestEffort: qosClasses[v1.PodQOSBestEffort],
   135          }
   136          m.getNodeAllocatable = getNodeAllocatable
   137          m.activePods = activePods
   138
   139          // update qos cgroup tiers on startup and in periodic intervals
   140          // to ensure desired state is in sync with actual state.
   141          go wait.Until(func() {
   142                  err := m.UpdateCgroups(logger)
   143                  if err != nil {
   144                          logger.Info("Failed to reserve QoS requests", "err", err)
   145                  }
   146          }, periodicQOSCgroupUpdateInterval, wait.NeverStop)
   147
   148          return nil
   149  }
   150
   151  // setHugePagesUnbounded ensures hugetlb is effectively unbounded
   152  func (m *qosContainerManagerImpl) setHugePagesUnbounded(cgroupConfig *CgroupConfig) error {
   153          hugePageLimit := map[int64]int64{}
   154          for _, pageSize := range libcontainercgroups.HugePageSizes() {
   155                  pageSizeBytes, err := units.RAMInBytes(pageSize)
   156                  if err != nil {
   157                          return err
   158                  }
   159                  hugePageLimit[pageSizeBytes] = int64(1 << 62)
   160          }
   161          cgroupConfig.ResourceParameters.HugePageLimit = hugePageLimit
   162          return nil
   163  }
   164
   165  func (m *qosContainerManagerImpl) setHugePagesConfig(configs map[v1.PodQOSClass]*CgroupConfig) error {
   166          for _, v := range configs {
   167                  if err := m.setHugePagesUnbounded(v); err != nil {
   168                          return err
   169                  }
   170          }
   171          return nil
   172  }
   173
   174  func (m *qosContainerManagerImpl) setCPUCgroupConfig(configs map[v1.PodQOSClass]*CgroupConfig) error {
   175          pods := m.activePods()
   176          burstablePodCPURequest := int64(0)
   177          reuseReqs := make(v1.ResourceList, 4)
   178          for i := range pods {
   179                  pod := pods[i]
   180                  qosClass := v1qos.GetPodQOS(pod)
   181                  if qosClass != v1.PodQOSBurstable {
   182                          // we only care about the burstable qos tier
   183                          continue
   184                  }
   185                  req := resource.PodRequests(pod, resource.PodResourcesOptions{
   186                          Reuse: reuseReqs,
   187                          // SkipPodLevelResources is set to false when PodLevelResources feature is enabled.
   188                          SkipPodLevelResources: !utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(kubefeatures.PodLevelResources),
   189                  })
   190                  if request, found := req[v1.ResourceCPU]; found {
   191                          burstablePodCPURequest += request.MilliValue()
   192                  }
   193          }
   194
   195          // make sure best effort is always 2 shares
   196          bestEffortCPUShares := uint64(MinShares)
   197          configs[v1.PodQOSBestEffort].ResourceParameters.CPUShares = &bestEffortCPUShares
   198
   199          // set burstable shares based on current observe state
   200          burstableCPUShares := MilliCPUToShares(burstablePodCPURequest)

    31  func (i *internalContainerLifecycleImpl) PreCreateContainer(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, containerConfig *runtimeapi.ContainerConfig) error {
    32          if i.cpuManager != nil {
    33                  allocatedCPUs := i.cpuManager.GetCPUAffinity(string(pod.UID), container.Name)
    34                  if !allocatedCPUs.IsEmpty() {
    35                          containerConfig.Linux.Resources.CpusetCpus = allocatedCPUs.String()
    36                  }
    37          }
    38
    39          if i.memoryManager != nil {
    40                  numaNodes := i.memoryManager.GetMemoryNUMANodes(logger, pod, container)
    41                  if numaNodes.Len() > 0 {
    42                          var affinity []string
    43                          for _, numaNode := range sets.List(numaNodes) {
    44                                  affinity = append(affinity, strconv.Itoa(numaNode))
    45                          }
    46                          containerConfig.Linux.Resources.CpusetMems = strings.Join(affinity, ",")
    47                  }
    48          }
    49
    50          return nil
    51  }

    28  type InternalContainerLifecycle interface {
    29          PreCreateContainer(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, containerConfig *runtimeapi.ContainerConfig) error
    30          PreStartContainer(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, containerID string) error
    31          PostStopContainer(logger klog.Logger, containerID string) error
    32  }
    33
    34  // Implements InternalContainerLifecycle interface.
    35  type internalContainerLifecycleImpl struct {
    36          cpuManager      cpumanager.Manager
    37          memoryManager   memorymanager.Manager
    38          topologyManager topologymanager.Manager
    39  }
    40
    41  func (i *internalContainerLifecycleImpl) PreStartContainer(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, containerID string) error {
    42          if i.cpuManager != nil {
    43                  i.cpuManager.AddContainer(logger, pod, container, containerID)
    44          }
    45
    46          if i.memoryManager != nil {
    47                  i.memoryManager.AddContainer(logger, pod, container, containerID)
    48          }
    49
    50          i.topologyManager.AddContainer(pod, container, containerID)
    51
    52          return nil
    53  }
    54
    55  func (i *internalContainerLifecycleImpl) PostStopContainer(logger klog.Logger, containerID string) error {
    56          return i.topologyManager.RemoveContainer(containerID)
    57  }

    26  // ResourceConfig holds information about all the supported cgroup resource parameters.
    27  type ResourceConfig struct {
    28          // Memory limit (in bytes).
    29          Memory *int64
    30          // CPU set (number of CPUs the cgroup has access to).
    31          CPUSet cpuset.CPUSet
    32          // CPU shares (relative weight vs. other containers).
    33          CPUShares *uint64
    34          // CPU hardcap limit (in usecs). Allowed cpu time in a given period.
    35          CPUQuota *int64
    36          // CPU quota period.
    37          CPUPeriod *uint64
    38          // HugePageLimit map from page size (in bytes) to limit (in bytes)
    39          HugePageLimit map[int64]int64
    40          // Maximum number of pids
    41          PidsLimit *int64
    42          // Unified for cgroup v2
    43          Unified map[string]string

    45
    46  // CgroupName is the abstract name of a cgroup prior to any driver specific conversion.
    47  // It is specified as a list of strings from its individual components, such as:
    48  // {"kubepods", "burstable", "pod1234-abcd-5678-efgh"}
    49  type CgroupName []string
    50
    51  // CgroupConfig holds the cgroup configuration information.
    52  // This is common object which is used to specify
    53  // cgroup information to both systemd and raw cgroup fs
    54  // implementation of the Cgroup Manager interface.
    55  type CgroupConfig struct {
    56          // Fully qualified name prior to any driver specific conversions.
    57          Name CgroupName
    58          // ResourceParameters contains various cgroups settings to apply.
    59          ResourceParameters *ResourceConfig
    60  }
    61
    62  // CgroupManager allows for cgroup management.
    63  // Supports Cgroup Creation ,Deletion and Updates.
    64  type CgroupManager interface {
    65          // Create creates and applies the cgroup configurations on the cgroup.
    66          // It just creates the leaf cgroups.
    67          // It expects the parent cgroup to already exist.
    68          Create(klog.Logger, *CgroupConfig) error
    69          // Destroy the cgroup.
    70          Destroy(klog.Logger, *CgroupConfig) error
    71          // Update cgroup configuration.
    72          Update(klog.Logger, *CgroupConfig) error
    73          // Validate checks if the cgroup is valid
    74          Validate(name CgroupName) error
    75          // Exists checks if the cgroup already exists
    76          Exists(name CgroupName) bool
    77          // Name returns the literal cgroupfs name on the host after any driver specific conversions.
    78          // We would expect systemd implementation to make appropriate name conversion.
    79          // For example, if we pass {"foo", "bar"}
    80          // then systemd should convert the name to something like
    81          // foo.slice/foo-bar.slice
    82          Name(name CgroupName) string
    83          // CgroupName converts the literal cgroupfs name on the host to an internal identifier.
    84          CgroupName(name string) CgroupName
    85          // Pids scans through all subsystems to find pids associated with specified cgroup.
    86          Pids(logger klog.Logger, name CgroupName) []int
    87          // ReduceCPULimits reduces the CPU CFS values to the minimum amount of shares.
    88          ReduceCPULimits(logger klog.Logger, cgroupName CgroupName) error
    89          // MemoryUsage returns current memory usage of the specified cgroup, as read from the cgroupfs.
    90          MemoryUsage(name CgroupName) (int64, error)
    91          // Get the resource config values applied to the cgroup for specified resource type
    92          GetCgroupConfig(name CgroupName, resource v1.ResourceName) (*ResourceConfig, error)
    93          // Set resource config for the specified resource type on the cgroup
    94          SetCgroupConfig(logger klog.Logger, name CgroupName, resourceConfig *ResourceConfig) error
    95          // Version of the cgroup implementation on the host
    96          Version() int
    97  }

   122          Destroy(logger klog.Logger, name CgroupName) error

   124          // ReduceCPULimits reduces the CPU CFS values to the minimum amount of shares.
   125          ReduceCPULimits(logger klog.Logger, name CgroupName) error
   126

   130          // IsPodCgroup returns true if the literal cgroupfs name corresponds to a pod

   137          GetPodCgroupConfig(pod *v1.Pod, resource v1.ResourceName) (*ResourceConfig, error)
   138

   140          SetPodCgroupConfig(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, resourceConfig *ResourceConfig) error

    30  // Names of the options, as part of the user interface.
    31  const (

    33          DistributeCPUsAcrossNUMAOption  string = "distribute-cpus-across-numa"

    40  var (
    41          alphaOptions = sets.New[string](

    45          betaOptions = sets.New[string](
    46                  DistributeCPUsAcrossNUMAOption,
    47                  PreferAlignByUnCoreCacheOption,

    49          stableOptions = sets.New[string](
    50                  FullPCPUsOnlyOption,
    51                  StrictCPUReservationOption,
    52          )

    54
    55  // CheckPolicyOptionAvailable verifies if the given option can be used depending on the Feature Gate Settings.
    56  // returns nil on success, or an error describing the failure on error.
    57  func CheckPolicyOptionAvailable(option string) error {
    58          if !alphaOptions.Has(option) && !betaOptions.Has(option) && !stableOptions.Has(option) {
    59                  return fmt.Errorf("unknown CPU Manager Policy option: %q", option)
    60          }
    61
    62          if alphaOptions.Has(option) && !utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(kubefeatures.CPUManagerPolicyAlphaOptions) {
    63                  return fmt.Errorf("CPU Manager Policy Alpha-level Options not enabled, but option %q provided", option)
    64          }
    65
    66          if betaOptions.Has(option) && !utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(kubefeatures.CPUManagerPolicyBetaOptions) {
    67                  return fmt.Errorf("CPU Manager Policy Beta-level Options not enabled, but option %q provided", option)
    68          }
    69
    70          // if the option is stable, we need no CPUManagerPolicy*Options feature gate check
    71          return nil
    72  }

    74  // StaticPolicyOptions holds the parsed value of the policy options, ready to be consumed internally.
    75  type StaticPolicyOptions struct {

    80          //
    81          // looking at https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/,
    82          // any possible naming scheme will lead to ambiguity to some extent.
    83          // We picked "pcpu" because it the established docs hints at vCPU already.
    84          FullPhysicalCPUsOnly bool
    85          // Flag to evenly distribute CPUs across NUMA nodes in cases where more
    86          // than one NUMA node is required to satisfy the allocation.
    87          DistributeCPUsAcrossNUMA bool
    88          // Flag to ensure CPUs are considered aligned at socket boundary rather than
    89          // NUMA boundary
    90          AlignBySocket bool
    91          // flag to enable extra allocation restrictions to spread
    92          // cpus (HT) on different physical core.
    93          // This is a preferred policy so do not throw error if they have to packed in one physical core.
    94          DistributeCPUsAcrossCores bool
    95          // Flag to remove reserved cores from the list of available cores
    96          StrictCPUReservation bool
    97          // Flag that makes best-effort to align CPUs to a uncorecache boundary
    98          // As long as there are CPUs available, pods will be admitted if the condition is not met.
    99          PreferAlignByUncoreCacheOption bool
